                                                                                                  Policy Forum


                                                                                  Natureza & Conservação 11(1):92-95, July 2013
                                                                                                           Copyright© 2013 ABECO
                                                                                                 Handling Editor: Jean Paul Metzger
Brazilian Journal of Nature Conservation                                                 http://dx.doi.org/10.4322/natcon.2013.015




Restoration Ecology in Brazil – Time to Step Out of the Forest

Gerhard E. Overbeck1*, Julia-Maria Hermann2, Bianca O. Andrade3, Ilsi I. Boldrini1,
Kathrin Kiehl4, Anita Kirmer5, Christiane Koch2, Johannes Kollmann2, Sebastian T. Meyer6,
Sandra C. Müller7, Carlos Nabinger8, Gabriele E. Pilger2, José Pedro P. Trindade9,
Eduardo Vélez-Martin10, Emer A. Walker2, Deonir G. Zimmermann11 & Valério D. Pillar7

1
    Departamento de Botânica, Universidade Federal do Rio Grande do Sul – UFRGS, Porto Alegre, RS, Brazil
2
    Restoration Ecology, Center of Life and Food Sciences Weihenstephan, Technische Universität München – TUM,
    ­ reising, Germany

3
    Programa de Pós-graduação em Botânica, Universidade Federal do Rio Grande do Sul – UFRGS, Porto Alegre, RS, Brazil
4
    Faculty of Agricultural Sciences and Landscape Architecture, Hochschule Osnabrück – University of Applied Sciences,
    Osnabrück, Germany
5
    Department of Nature Conservation and Landscape Planning, Anhalt University of Applied Sciences, Bernburg, Germany
6
    Terrestrial Ecology, Center of Life and Food Sciences Weihenstephan, Technische Universität München – TUM, Freising,
    Germany
7
    Departamento de Ecologia, Universidade Federal do Rio Grande do Sul – UFRGS, Porto Alegre, RS, Brazil
8
    Departamento de Plantas Forrageiras e Agrometeorologia, Universidade Federal do Rio Grande do Sul – UFRGS, Porto
    Alegre, RS, Brazil
9
    EMBRAPA Pecuária Sul, Bagé, RS, Brazil
10
    Programa de Pós-graduação em Ecologia, Universidade Federal do Rio Grande do Sul – UFRGS, Porto Alegre, RS, Brazil
11
     Parque Nacional Aparados da Serra, ICMBio, Cambará do Sul, RS, Brazil




As global trends in conversion and degradation of natural       development of biome-specific knowledge and technologies
and near-natural ecosystems continue, restoration ecology       in ecological restoration (Brazil 2012).
is considered an increasingly important area of research,
                                                                Restoration ecology thus has made considerable progress
while ecological restoration becomes an essential part of
                                                                in Brazil, both as a scientific discipline and as an area of
environmental policy. Restoration not only contributes
                                                                applied technology and environmental policy, contributing
to biodiversity conservation, but also to maintenance of        to biodiversity conservation and sustainable land use in
important ecological services for humankind, even though        Brazil. However, up to now, the focus of restoration ecology
the relation between species diversity and ecosystem            has been principally on forest vegetation or on the woody
services is complex and generalizations difficult (MEA          component of non-forest vegetation types such as Cerrado
2005, Bullock et al. 2011a). In the past decades, restoration   (Kageyama et al. 2003b, Zamith & Scarano 2006, Durigan
ecology has emerged as a new scientific discipline in Brazil    2007, Sampaio et al. 2007), while open vegetation types
(e.g. Kageyama et al. 2003a), and the restoration of degraded   have been largely neglected. Even though the largest part of
lands was included as one of the principles in the Brazilian    Brazil’s natural vegetation is forest, different types of open
National Environmental Policy Law in 1981 (Brazil 1981).        vegetation cover substantial parts of the country, such as
More recently, the Brazilian Ministry of the Environment        in the Cerrado (e.g. ‘Campo limpo’, ‘Campos rupestres’),
announced a plan to create, until 2015, two reference centers   in the Pantanal, in parts of the Mata Atlântica (‘Campos
for restoration of degraded ecosystems within each of the six   de altitude’), and in the ‘Campos sulinos’ (South Brazilian
biomes of the country, with the objective of promoting the      grasslands). The latter, included in the Pampa biome and
                                                                the southern part of the Mata Atlântica (Atlantic Forest)
*Send correspondence to: Gerhard Overbeck
                                                                biome, are the focus of this contribution. Just as other regions
  Departamento de Botânica, Universidade Federal do Rio
  Grande do Sul – UFRGS, CEP 91501-970, Porto Alegre, RS,       dominated by tropical and subtropical grasslands around
  Brazil.                                                       the world (Bond & Parr 2010), grasslands in southern Brazil
  E-mail: gerhard.overbeck@ufrgs.br                             have not been, until quite recently, considered as priority
Restoration Ecology – Time to Step Out of the Forest                                                                             93


for research and conservation (Overbeck et al. 2007), and           These degradation processes not only lead to losses of
restoration of these grasslands has not been an issue so far.       biodiversity, but also reduce important ecosystem services
This seems to be principally a consequence of a general             provided by grasslands, such as carbon storage in soil, erosion
lack of concern with conservation of non-forest vegetation          control, water infiltration into the soil and forage production
in Brazil, a bias probably induced by the massive rates of          (White et al., 2000 and references therein, Tornquist & Bayer,
deforestation seen in the Amazon and Atlantic forest biomes         2009; Pillar et al., 2012). It is mostly unknown to which
in the 20th century, when ecological consciousness emerged.         extent these functions or services can return to near-natural
                                                                    conditions under unassisted recovery, given that former
The grasslands in southern Brazil (i.e. in the states of Rio
                                                                    grassland areas become available for such recovery following
Grande do Sul, Santa Catarina and Paraná) are very rich in
                                                                    logging of pine or eucalypt plantations, or cessation of
biodiversity (Bencke 2009, Boldrini 2009). For grasslands
                                                                    intensive arable use (Figure 1). Grassland restoration is
in Rio Grande do Sul alone (i.e. considering areas included
                                                                    especially important within priority areas for conservation
both in the Pampa and the Mata Atlântica biome), about
                                                                    or close to conservation units. The obligation to restore areas
2,500 vascular plant species have been recorded. That is five
                                                                    in the ‘Reserva Legal’, defined in the Brazilian Forest Code
times the number of native tree species occurring in the
                                                                    (‘Código Florestal’), on the other hand, turns restoration
state, and 20% of these species are endemics (Boldrini et al.,
                                                                    into an important topic throughout the country, and thus
in prep.). Grasslands composed of native species support
                                                                    makes new concepts for widespread grassland restoration
traditional livestock production, which still is the basis of the   necessary. In this context, it is important to recognize that

landscapes in southern Brazil. However, in the past decades         the most essential ecosystem functions (Aerts & Honnay
the grassland area has decreased rapidly, principally due to        2011, Bullock et al. 2011a). In most grasslands, forage
land use transformation (Crawshaw et al. 2007), causing             production is one of these. However, it may not always be
not only direct losses, but also increasing fragmentation           possible to restore the full ‘functionality’ of the ecosystem.
of grassland remnants. The Pampa biome has suffered a
complete replacement of original vegetation in 54% of its           Initiatives for restoration of grassland ecosystems in southern
area. Together with the Caatinga biome, which holds the             Brazil face five serious challenges:

thus is listed second to the Mata Atlântica biome regarding

throughout Rio Grande do Sul (Cordeiro & Hasenack 2009).
While grasslands have remained relatively intact in regions               this is often unknown or not acknowledged by
with less potential for intensive agriculture, they have been             landowners;
almost completely destroyed in other regions, mostly due to           •   No experience on grassland restoration is available in
soybean or rice production. More recently, afforestation with
                                                                          Brazil, and approaches developed for other vegetation
non-native tree species (often eucalypts or pines) has led to
                                                                          types most likely are not appropriate, as structure and
substantial landscape changes in regions with poorer soils.
                                                                          dynamics of grasslands and forests are very different.
Furthermore, even grasslands under grazing management
are threatened by different degradation processes, such as                For example, plantation of trees and establishment
invasion by non-native species. It has been estimated that                of forest nuclei as a way to attract animal dispersers
the most problematic species, the C4 grass Eragrostis plana,              and thus promote succession processes are common
introduced from Africa, occurs in more than one million                   techniques for restoration of forest, but unsuitable for
hectares in Rio Grande do Sul (Medeiros & Focht 2007).                    grassland vegetation;




Figure 1. Managed natural grassland (reference ecosystem for restoration) and examples of three degradation stages (abandoned
pasture, logged plantation and abandoned arable land) in the highlands of southern Brazil, Rio Grande do Sul (Photos: J.-M. Hermann,
Jan. 2012).

         of even the most common native grassland species,            that new concepts and improved experience in restoration
         while seeds from non-native species (some of them            of non-forest vegetation can help reducing and eventually
         invasive) are easily available. This is a serious obstacle   stopping further losses in biodiversity, ecosystem services and
         for any grassland restoration project;                       characteristic landscapes, not only in the ‘Campos sulinos’,
                                                                      but also in other parts of Brazil. Grassland restoration needs
     •   The notion that disturbances like grazing and fire are       a new set of concepts and techniques, and conservation of
         vital for grassland maintenance and diversity under          grasslands, in contrast to many other Brazilian ecosystems,
         climatic conditions suitable for forest succession is not    often depends on suitable management to be successful in
         widely recognized in Brazilian conservation policy.          the long run.
         Introduction of management, such as grazing, in              So, is there a conflict between the aims of grassland and
         grassland restoration projects, as known from many           forest restoration? We do not think so, as the choice of a
         other regions of the world, is something not discussed       reference system that helps to define restoration targets
         so far. Vegetation management for conservation is            and methods is not an arbitrary choice between forest and
         still rarely considered by the public or in conservation     grassland (or any other ecosystem type, for this matter), but
         policy;                                                      a question of the ecological context, the specific conditions
                                                                      and history of the site to be restored. In consequence,
     •   The necessity of restoration of grassland vegetation is      different restoration objectives will exist within biomes
         still poorly recognized in Brazil, just as the benefits of   or even vegetation types. No standard procedures exist for
         restoration for large-scale conservation of biodiversity     restoration, but restoration strategies that consider the full
         and ecosystem services.                                      diversity of Brazilian ecosystems and their characteristics
                                                                      will contribute significantly to conservation of biodiversity
The good news is that many examples of successful grassland           and ecosystem functioning throughout the country.
restoration have been developed in other countries, for
grasslands under a wide range of climatic conditions, different       Acknowledgments
management regimes and degradation processes (e.g.
Packard & Mutel 2005, Kiehl et al. 2010, Bakker et al. 2012).         This contribution is a result of the Workshop “Scientific

2010). Thus, the experiences made in grassland restoration
around the world could be used as a basis for developing              References
and testing grassland restoration in Brazil, even though we

the landscape context needs to be considered. Restoration             Boldrini II, 2009. A flora dos campos do Rio Grande do Sul.

and minimize possible negative impacts from other land
uses in adjacent areas, such as spread of invasive species.           Bond WJ & Parr CL, 2010. Beyond the forest edge: Ecology,
